DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFF MARK ROBINSON III'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND FOR SANCTIONS. DEFENDANTS, WITHIN 14 DAYS (1) MUST PROVIDE INFORMATION AND DOCUMENTS CONCERNING COMMUNICATIONS MADE BY OR TO INDIVIDUALS INVOLVED IN INVESTIGATING OR DISCIPLINING PLAINTIFF, OTHER THAN OUTSIDE COUNSEL AFTER 1/1/10 INVOLVING THE SUBJECT OF PLAINTIFF'S DISCIPLINE OR THE CONDUCT ON THE BASIS OF WHICH HE WAS DISCIPLINED AND, (2) MUST PROVIDE DOCUMENTS WHICH PLACED A DISTRICT EMPLOYEE ON ADMINISTRATIVE LEAVE OR EXTENDED OR ENDED SUCH LEAVE SUBSEQUENT TO 1/1/06 WHERE THE GROUNDS FOR DISCIPLINE INVOLVED CHARGES OF THE KIND OF OFFENSES WITH WHICH PLAINTIFF WAS CHARGED AND WITH THE NAMES OF THE DISCIPLINED EMPLOYEES AND WITNESSES AND COMPLAINANTS REDACTED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO FOLLOW-UP DISCOVERY IF APPROPRIATE. TO THE EXTENT DEFENDANTS HAVE WITHHELD ANY RESPONSIVE, RELEVANT DOCUMENTS ON GROUNDS OF ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGE THAT ARE NOT COMMUNICATIONS BY OR TO AN OUTSIDE ATTORNEY, SUCH DOCUMENTS MUST BE IDENTIFIED IN ACCORDANCE WITH CCP SECTION 2031.240(B). THE COURT DID NOT AWARD SANCTIONS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED (301/PJB)

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. RYAN L. HICKS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MICHAEL S. SORGEN, TEL. NO. 956-1360 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MATTHEW R. HALLORAN & ATTY. RICHARD S. DIESTEL OF THE BLEDSOE LLP, TEL. NO. 981-5411 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT SIRRON NORRIS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT SIRRON NORRIS' Motion and Special Motion To Strike Slapp Lawsuit IS DENIED. THIS MOTION EFFECTIVELY SEEKS RECONSIDERATION OF THE COURT'S DENIAL ON THE MERITS OF DEFENDANT'S FIRST ANTI-SLAPP MOTION BEYOND THE TIME PERMITTED AND WITHOUT POINTING TO NEW CIRCUMSTANCES OTHER THAN A DESIRE TO BRING A BETTER SUPPORTED MOTION BY NEWLY RETAINED COUNSEL. ALTHOUGH AN ANTI-SLAPP MOTION MAY BE BROUGHT WITHIN 60 DAYS OF AN AMENDED PLEADING EVEN WHERE THE RELEVANT ALLEGATIONS WERE NOT NEW. THAT DOES NOT MEAN THE AMENDED COMPLAINT ALLOWS A SECOND BITE AT THE SAME APPLE BASED ON ALLEGATIONS THAT DID NOT CHANGE IN A WAY MATERIAL TO THIS MOTION WHICH SEEKS THE SAME RELIEF PREVIOUSLY DENIED. REQUEST FOR SANCTION IS DENIED. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter, Carol A. Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
